title: The man who fixes umbrellas
summary: Describe a small repair worker who mends broken umbrellas and explain why fixing things matters more than throwing them away.
topic: A person who repairs broken umbrellas in the city
prompt: Write 8–10 short sentences about a person who repairs umbrellas (or another small repair worker). Describe where they work, what they do, and why you bring your broken things to them. Reflect on repairing instead of throwing away. Use present simple, past simple, and 'and', 'but', 'because'.
hintStarters: On a corner near the market there is a man who…
I take it to him because…
sampleText: On a corner near the market there is a man who fixes umbrellas. He sits on a small stool every day. He has a box of tools and many old metal ribs. People bring him broken umbrellas after every storm. He looks at the umbrella and finds the problem quickly. Then he bends the metal, sews the cloth, and tests it in the air. A repair costs very little money. Last week my umbrella broke in the rain. The metal rib snapped near the top. A new umbrella is cheap, but I did not want to throw it away. So I took it to the man on the corner. He fixed it in ten minutes and we talked about the weather. Now my umbrella opens like new. I like to repair things because it makes less waste. The man says he fixes many umbrellas, but fewer people come now. Most people buy a new one and throw the old one away. I think his small work is very useful for the city.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một người đàn ông sửa ô

sampleAnnotations.phrase: He sits on a small stool
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Ông ngồi trên một chiếc ghế đẩu nhỏ
tìm ra vấn đề nhanh chóng
cái ô của tôi bị hỏng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: did not want to throw it away
He fixed it in ten minutes
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Ông sửa nó trong mười phút
nó tạo ra ít rác thải hơn
ít người đến hơn bây giờ

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Third-person singular -s with 'he' and 'a man who...'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi chủ ngữ là 'he' hoặc 'a man who', động từ thêm -s: 'a man who fixes', 'he sits', 'he bends', 'he sews', 'he says'. Đây là điểm dễ quên khi tả công việc của một người khác.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: When the subject is 'he' or 'a man who...', the verb takes -s: 'a man who fixes', 'he sits', 'he bends', 'he sews', 'he says'. This -s is easy to drop when describing another person's work.

sampleTranslationVi: Ở một góc đường gần chợ có một người đàn ông sửa ô. Ông ngồi trên một chiếc ghế đẩu nhỏ mỗi ngày. Ông có một hộp dụng cụ và nhiều nan kim loại cũ. Người ta mang ô hỏng đến cho ông sau mỗi cơn bão. Ông nhìn cái ô và tìm ra vấn đề nhanh chóng. Rồi ông uốn cong miếng kim loại, khâu lại lớp vải, và thử nó trong không khí. Một lần sửa chỉ tốn rất ít tiền. Tuần trước cái ô của tôi bị hỏng trong mưa. Nan kim loại gãy ở gần đỉnh. Một cái ô mới thì rẻ, nhưng tôi không muốn vứt nó đi. Nên tôi mang nó đến người đàn ông ở góc đường. Ông sửa nó trong mười phút và chúng tôi nói chuyện về thời tiết. Bây giờ cái ô của tôi mở ra như mới. Tôi thích sửa đồ vì nó tạo ra ít rác thải hơn. Người đàn ông nói ông sửa nhiều ô, nhưng bây giờ ít người đến hơn. Hầu hết mọi người mua cái mới và vứt cái cũ đi. Tôi nghĩ công việc nhỏ của ông rất hữu ích cho thành phố.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple form: 'There is a man who ___ umbrellas.'
options: fix
fixing
fixes
fixed
mcQuestions.explanation: 'a man who' is third-person singular, so the verb takes -es: 'fixes'.
mcQuestions.hint: 'A man who' acts like 'he' — what ending does the verb need?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the past simple for last week?
options: Last week my umbrella breaked in the rain.
Last week my umbrella break in the rain.
Last week my umbrella is broke in the rain.
Last week my umbrella broke in the rain.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'broke' is the irregular past simple of 'break'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'but' correctly to show a contrast?
options: A new umbrella is cheap, but I did not want to throw it away.
A new umbrella is cheap, but throw away I did not want it.
A new umbrella cheap is, but I not want throw it away.
But a new umbrella is cheap I did not throw it away want.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'but' joins two full clauses; the second is 'I did not want to throw it away'.
mcQuestions.hint: Both clauses after 'but' need a subject and a verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best explains why you like to repair things?
options: I like to repair things because less waste makes.
I like to repair things because it makes less waste.
I like to repair things so less waste it.
Because less waste I like repair things to.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because' is followed by a full clause: 'it makes less waste'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple: 'He ___ the metal and sews the cloth.'
options: bend
bending
bends
bended
mcQuestions.hint: The subject is 'he' — what ending does the verb need?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses the past simple about the repair?
options: He fix it in ten minutes.
He fixed it in ten minute.
He is fixed it in ten minutes.
He fixed it in ten minutes.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'fixed' is the correct past simple and 'minutes' is plural.
mcQuestions.hint: Which option is a clean past-simple verb with plural 'minutes'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence sounds the most natural describing the umbrella now?
options: Now my umbrella opens like new.
Now my umbrella open like new.
Now my umbrella opens like newly.
Now my umbrella opens like new download.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'my umbrella opens' (singular -s) and 'like new' are both correct and natural.
mcQuestions.hint: The subject 'umbrella' is singular — what ending does the verb need?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is the most natural and on-topic?
options: People brings him broken umbrellas after every storm.
People bring him broken umbrellas after every storm.
People bring him broken umbrella after every storms.
People bring him broken umbrellas after every storm to upload.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'people bring' (plural, no -s) and 'every storm' (singular) are correct.
mcQuestions.hint: Is 'people' singular or plural, and is it 'every storm' or 'every storms'?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly describes the change in his work?
options: He fixes many umbrellas, but fewer people comes now.
He fix many umbrellas, but fewer people come now.
He fixes many umbrellas, but fewer people come now.
He fixes many umbrellas, but less people come now.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'he fixes' (-s), 'people come' (no -s), and 'fewer' (for countable people) are all correct.
mcQuestions.hint: Use 'fewer' for countable people and check both verbs.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is the most natural way to give your opinion at the end?
options: I think his small work are very useful for the city.
I think his small work is very usefully for the city.
I think his small work is very useful for the city machine.
I think his small work is very useful for the city.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'work is' (singular) and 'very useful' (adjective) are both correct and natural.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb after 'work' and use an adjective, not an adverb.

criticalThinkingQuestion: When something small breaks, like an umbrella, why do many people buy a new one instead of fixing the old one, and is that a good thing?
